data.narrative:
Elroy Air is a Series B stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, currently valued at $400M post-money following $116M in total capital raised. The broader peer universe consists of 7 private companies in the drones and autonomous space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Manna Aero, and Asylon Robotics. No public comparables exist in this narrow subsector.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the company's Series B positioning. Applying a typical Series B to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the $400M last post-money valuation implies a forward valuation of $760M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrowly-filtered comparables—while three same-sector peers exist, none met filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier status simultaneously. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $646M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $760M. This range reflects the single applicable methodology and carries material uncertainty given the limited peer set and reliance on stage-based assumptions rather than market-validated comparable transactions. Key uncertainties include whether the 1.9x multiplier appropriately reflects this subsector's risk profile and whether future-stage investor appetite will materialize at the implied valuation. Additional transaction data from adjacent-stage drones companies would refine confidence in this range.
